summary: We recommend recording the empty pre-deploy state of the outcomes-mcp Cloudflare Worker as the C1 runroc rather than waiting for C2 to populate a build history because the absence is itself the audit finding. The tradeoff is that workers_builds_list_builds was not invoked this tick since it requires a workerId and none exists yet but workers_list against account e6294e3ea89f8207af387d459824aaae returned the full 15-worker inventory with no outcomes prefix entry. The runroc outcomes runrocs C1-builds json captures the account id the full worker inventory and the interpretation that C2 wrangler deploy is the next Track C step.

claims.statement: workers_list against active account e6294e3ea89f8207af387d459824aaae returned 15 workers and none of them are named outcomes-mcp or any outcomes prefix variant which is the expected pre-deploy baseline.

citations.title: C1 runroc captures the workers_list response and the empty outcomes-mcp finding
Cloudflare wrangler deploy reference that C2 will use to create the worker

claims.caveats: workers_list operates at account scope; if the operator deploys to a different account in the future the audit must be re-run against that account id before drawing conclusions.

claims.statement: workers_builds_list_builds was intentionally not invoked this tick because the tool requires a workerId and no outcomes-mcp worker exists yet so calling it would have returned an error not a useful empty result.

citations.title: Cloudflare API reference for the Workers Builds list endpoint that requires a worker id path parameter
QUEUE md row for C2 names wrangler deploy as the prerequisite for a populated builds history

claims.caveats: If C1 is re-run after C2 lands a deploy the workerId will resolve and a real build history will populate the runroc; this tick captures the baseline only.

claims.statement: the C1 runroc is structured as a json object with account block tool used field workers_present_in_account list and a next_action field pointing at C2 so a downstream reader can audit the baseline without re-running the MCP query.

citations.title: C1 runroc full content for downstream auditors
QUEUE md runroc_path column for C1 names outcomes runrocs C1-builds json

claims.caveats: The runroc lives under outcomes runrocs which is gitignored at the directory level; the runroc files themselves are force-added per tick which is the existing pattern from B4.

open_questions: Should the C1 runroc be re-fetched on every Track C tick or treated as a one-shot baseline that C2 supersedes?
methodology_notes: workers_list called via the Cloudflare MCP Docker gateway under profile reinforcement_data_engineering with active_account set to e6294e3ea89f8207af387d459824aaae. No worker named outcomes-mcp or any outcomes prefix variant was present. The runroc captures the full 15-worker inventory for the auditor's benefit.
